Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington
Full_Review: The lift is quite small we were 2 and fit in quite snug with our hand luggage not sure how we would have managed if we each had a large suitcase and the staircase is also quite narrow should one choose to avoid the lift   The rooms are tiny but have everything you could need for a short stay The bed is extremely comfortable I felt like I was sleeping on a combination of clouds and marshmallows and the TV is attached to a bracket on the wall that swivels out for comfortable viewing whilst in bed The shower pressure is amazing and the bathroom is fairly roomy when taking into consideration the tiny room size The breakfast room is also quite small but again covers everything you would need Both continental and full English available a variety of bread and condiments along with a variety of herbal teas Staff are also very helpful and friendly WiFi is freely available and fairly good speed and the location is incredible 2 min walk to Earl s Court Station with Byron s on the way

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington

Full_Review: We were given a room on the basement floor and it was the worst hotel experience in my life The cell phone signal tended to die out a lot in the room I had to constantly go to the ground floor to make phone calls The Wi Fi didn t work 90 percent of the time and when it did work it was only for a minute or two Due to the room being basically underground it was constantly dark and the lamps didn t give sufficient light The constant semi darkness was extremely depressing To be fair there was a window in the room but it overlooked some rubbish bins or something like that in a basement and there were constantly people poking around those bins so we had to keep the curtains closed which again led to the problem of not enough light And the absolutely worst thing is that either the guests or the stuff of the hotel used this corner of the basement as a place for smoking and we constantly had the terrible smell of smoke all over our room My mother having asthma had to double her inhaler usage and I had to take anti allergy pills   Good location amazing choice of breakfast foods AC luxury shower

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington
Full_Review: We had a problem with the air conditioning in our room and the hotel was full so they could not move us to another room However they really tried to make sure we are comfortable so brought a fan to the room and the next day even though it was Sunday they had someone come in and fix it   The hotel is small with a real nice feel of a victorian house to it it s in an old victorian building the rooms were renovated and are modernly designed The whole street is a row of victorian houses with a few hotels in the adjacent buildings The staff were extremely nice and very helpful It feels like they really care that you have a good experience There are small things you get for free like water bottles and cookies in the room The rooms are small but well arranged and were perfect for us Breakfast was a full English type breakfast with lots of selection We ended up eating too much Location is great It is about 2 min walk to the Earls Court tube station from which you can easily get to any of the interesting places in London There are a few restaurants and bars up the street which was also great for getting a bite or a drink and a supermarket at the closest street corner Getting there from Heathrow is just one 45 min underground trip from Heathrow or about a 30 min cab ride from there We are definitely coming back if they have vacancy the next time around

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington
Full_Review: Our room was on Lower Ground floor which is basically the basement The room was therefore very dark and also very small The breakfast was ok but the buffet was not that well stocked which meant we had to ask for more glasses juices eggs etc The wi fi did not work all the time   Good location with only a few hundred meters to Earls Court station Good pubs nearby The beds were comfortable Helpful staff

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington
Full_Review: Not consistently giving us face cloth instead of having to ask for them after the first day not re filling the water bottles every day a refrigerator would have helped out a lot due to there was plenty of space for it   The bed was my favorite part of the stay comfy and soft Clean room bathroom clean free bottle of water coffee and a cookie Wifi is good in the room Staff helpful and friendly they even ordered a pizza for us because we have no idead how to The location is literally a 5 min walk from the Tube grocery store restaurants star bucks and laundry mat Safe neighborhood

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington
Full_Review: This was the worst hotel I ve ever stayed in during my life The first room they gave me lower ground floor means basement was tiny dark and dingy and right by the steps from the street down to the staff entrance so the staff had a nice view of my room and me The internet connection wouldn t work in that room so next day they moved me to a room on the 2nd floor which was marginally brighter but still tiny and the day after the internet was down for the whole day a disaster as I had urgent work to do The room was noisy you could hear people in other rooms using the shower and running water The bed was seriously uncomfortable At the prices they charge it was a total disgrace How it ever got a 4 rating is way beyond me   The staff members were fairly helpful

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington
Full_Review: Well I think it s a joke that room we were given was classed as double I have never stayed in a smaller room To top it off the aircon only works in half of it even thougt the whole room is 3 x 2 metres To illustrate I couldn t fully open my suitcase anywhere in the room Definitely would not recommend this hotel   We were able to leave our luggage there prior to check in

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington
Full_Review: Receptionist should offer first rooms upstairs Rooms in basement are unacceptable Upstairs is nice and convenient   Localization is great Upstairs rooms are nice and comfortable

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington
Full_Review: the boiler or AC makes a lot of noise so it was very hard to sleep with the constant humming and could hear next door neighbours through the wall like they were In the same room In addition air quality is poor also because the room Is a bit moldy   Location next to Earl s court station Affordable The basement flat that has its own entry

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington
Full_Review: The room was designated as an upgrade It as extremely small for 2 people The temperature control sporadic at best  The staff was very friendly and tried to be helpful getting our temperature under control Location was great

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington
Full_Review: Although this can t be changed the rooms were a bit on the small side But to be fair they moved us to a bigger room without any fuss  The staff were amazingly helpful the hotel was very clean The location was excellent

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington
Full_Review: For the price I d expect breakfast to be included Even if it was just continental and extra for full English  The staff were excellent Very helpful and cheerful Room was small but had everything you could need Even had a iron and ironing board which I thought was a nice touch

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington
Full_Review: Terrible beds ridiculously soft saggy old mattresses with springs that I could feel digging into me Also shorter than a regular bed would have been terrible for anyone taller than me I am 5 ft 5 and my feet were on the end of it In addition there was what I can only describe as a super thermal winter duvet in summer making it so incredibly hot that I could not sleep under it even with the noisy air con set to very cold I literally have never known a duvet like it It belongs in the arctic There were no sheets either so it was either super hot duvet or nothing The bed and duvet meant that I had an absolutely horrific night of barely any sleep Room was also unbelievably tiny and the corridors etc are the same giving it a bit of a b and B feel Considering we paid 180 it was not good at all and breakfast would have added another 30 but we decided against it Some staff were unfriendly but then perhaps I wasn t in the best mood to judge them kindly after such a horrible sleepless night No mini bar or fridge either I would not stay there again   Very clean room nice bathroom some staff were OK

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington
Full_Review: The Russian German female MGR was terrible and cold and downright Gestapo like zero pleasant THEY OVERCHARGED Me on room nightly fee waiting for that adjustment TRIED to charge me for the two morning breakfasts this was to be included and also my POINTS to my BEST WESTERN member card the Russian MGR balked at giving that to me then agreed HOSPITALITY from her and customer service was not pleasant Leak in the room from rain which did stop but   loved some of the staff Louie was wonderful and helpful I also liked the other late desk concierge his name He was black and really helpful when i had dinner delivered Also breakfast was amazing BUT they tried to charge me extra as this was included IN ROOM FEE RATE

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington
Full_Review: The room was tiny but it did had everything I needed   Cleanliness excellent breakfast very good stuff always helpful excellent location in the terms of public transportation

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington
Full_Review: The only downside of the hotel was the matress of bed which was too soft   The hotel is very well located just 4 min walking distance to the tube station It s also located in a quiet road The room was clean and well decorated I was first alocated a basement room however I couldn t open the window The hotel staff moved me to another room which was very nice I also enjoyed the continental breakfast with warm croissants

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington
Full_Review: The bed It reminded me of the first innerspring mattress my parents purchased 65 years ago They thought it was great I almost feared I would roll out of bed during the night   I liked the location of the hotel It was on the Piccadilly line convenient for LHR and Eurostar I enjoyed staying in the Earls Court neighborhood The hotel was on a quiet street and the tube and buses were just a block away The TV line up was so good I sometimes found it hard to break away from it and leave my room

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington
Full_Review: Single room is very small For example opening a normal suitcase is possible in only one place and there it blocks either bathroom or hallway door The bed is also very small I am 180 cm 5 feet 11 inches and I doubt that anybody taller than me could sleep in it There is a noticeable draft from the window even in mid May Considering the bed is next to the window during window this would be very uncomfortable Loaning a plug adaptor costs more than a new adaptor from a shop Sadly I found this out when checking out The info folder tells about the possibility of a lending one and need for a deposit but not that there is a fee for it Neither did the front desk person lending me the unit   Hotel s location is very nice very close to Earl s Court station The hotel has been recently renovated and in nice condition

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington
Full_Review: I had reserved online through booking com two rooms a double room for my parents and a single room for a friend of theirs I had booked both rooms as refundable on purpose if anything came up in the four months prior to their stay Reading more reviews on the hotel in different portals I got worried about feedback on the size of the rooms and decided to go and take a look myself as I wanted my parents to be as comfortable as possible The lady at reception was friendly and showed me two rooms the size of the double room was ok but the single room felt really claustrophobic Besides there was nothing warm or welcoming abt the place and I absolutely did not want my parents to stay there When I got home I discovered that I had made a mistake and only the double room was fully refundable while the single room was on a non refundable rate I called booking com right away and asked for advice they were very friendly and told me that most hotels agree to returning the money nonetheless which is in their discretion especially if there is still 4months to resell the room and the booking had just been made a few days ago I phoned the hotel straight away and they told me they would not be willing to let me off the hook I then walked by another day in the hope to have a better dialogue in person and the answer remained the same I did find a hotel nearby the Cranley hotel bina gardens for about the same price which was absolutely delightful with tasteful big rooms where my parents had a wonderful stay I can only recommend to anyone looking for a room to look elsewhere it s unbelievable how different a package you can get for the same price

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington
Full_Review: Internet signal was almost none on the 4th floor The location is convenient but the Eiffel Tower area needs a big renovation and maintenance   Bed was very comfty hair dryer worked very good great shower pressure and hot water

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington
Full_Review: The air con seemed to be struggling with the very high temperatures we were experiencing   Everything had been done to a good quality from the front steps to the sink in the bathroom

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington
Full_Review: The room was very cramped I booked a triple room which was in fact a double room with a camp bed squashed in This left little room for 3 girls getting ready for heading out for the night A pair of dirty socks were left on the floor when we arrived and the girl who stayed in the camp bed came home with a few bites from bed bugs   The location was superb Only a 2 minute form Earls Court underground stop and only 1 or 2 stops away from everything The staff were pleasant and helpful The en suite bathroom was lovely and very nice shower Plenty of towels and toiletries

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington
Full_Review: The bathroom was like a sauna heating on full blast and no way to turn it off The lift is too small to take a pram hall ways are too narrow I had to walk like a crab with pram and car seat and the room is small   Clean and quiet location for London

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington
Full_Review: The room was dull dirty carpet and disgusting chair covered in stains The was a stale smell of old perfume and the heating and air conditioning didn t work properly The shower head was old and the bathroom small I don t think this hotel deserves 4 star status   Good location friendly staff

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington
Full_Review: There were many small glitches during my stay locked out of room several times noisy other guests nearby walked in on when relaxing on bed etc etc BUT I must stress that staff responded very well and everything was sorted to my satisfaction I would say more a 3 than 4   Location 1 minute from tube Good rate Staff pleasant and helpful

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington
Full_Review: The bed s are overly soft One side of the bed was over used compared to the other side My first room s TV was situated so that you couldn t watch from bed Only from a chair   The breakfast was very good Staff very attentive and flexible

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington
Full_Review: The rooms were small but we were ok with that The bathroom especially the toilet was most annoying it was against the wall and it was almost impossible to sit   Loved the location and friendly staff The price was right also

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington
Full_Review: stayed at this hotel many times before was very disappointed with room we had room 8 as was in basement first night could not sleep properly a very noisy air condition unit and a lot of slamming of doors very uncomfortable bed as it had dip in middle i suggest a new mattress wouldn t go amiss  it was clean

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington
Full_Review: room to small it should be used as a single hardly any floor space had to climb over husband to get in and out of bed as it was against a wall no reading lamp or bedside table one side of bed not enough storage walls needed painting bathroom very small we had a shower in ours it was so small if you dropped your sponge you had to open the door to bend down and pick it up even breakfast room was small and they were very slow replenishing the cooked food  in a good location for the underground

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington
Full_Review: The friendly staff especially one of the breakfast staff can t remember her name but she had red hair it was her birthday the day after my daughters on the 18th Feb she was extremely helpful nice friendly person to chat too Would definitely stay again

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington
Full_Review: More information should be provided for parking It was a nightmare arriving late and trying to find somewhere for 48hrs parking   The rooms were clean the facilities all that you would expect for the money The location was excellent very quiet and very handy for the Underground or walking to Kensington The staff were superb Very friendly and informative

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington
Full_Review: The rooms are as always in London small You can not do much other than placing a bag A desk close to the Bed a small tv and slow internet   Good hotel breakfast was good Room was clean

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington
Full_Review: The first night we were in room 111 It was really too small seemed like a single room made up for two the next day we could change the room and it was much better The WiFi connection was ok but not really good   Specially Ms Ieva from the Front Desk was outstanding Seems she was always here In the morning at 10 00 or in the evening at 9 00 She answered all our questions and was really helpful Great Service

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington
Full_Review: 1st room not clean BUT they changed us right away 2nd was all fine i guess was a random thing wifi need to insert a new code every day wifi signal on 1st room not that strong on 2nd all fine room small same as in most hotels around in that area  Area full of restaurants too close to tube Earls Court and close to easyjet drop points Hotel staff all very kind serving and with positive energy especially Pascal 1st time i recall all receptionists names

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington
Full_Review: Room was sweltering on arrival Tony rang to ask if all was OK and soon sorted the problem  Staff were great Iena answering daft tourist questions with a good sense of humour Arl in breakfast cheerful hardworking and keen to give us a good breakfast which was always achieved All staff were good

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington
Full_Review: The Bed was too soft The air conditioning smelled funny and made a noise so was unusable  The staff were efficient and mostly friendly I didn t have breakfast so cannot comment

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington
Full_Review: 1 My room handed to me after 2 30 afternoon because I told the hotel my arrival time will be between 1 to 2 pm and I has been waiting around 40 minutes until my room get ready 2 No WiFi because the signal was very weak 3 No fridge dirty carpet cover devote was to old and ripped 4 The bead too small for couples 5 Its impossible to use the basin because its too close the toilet set 6 The kettle and tea cups kept inside the wardrobe

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington
Full_Review: Staff in the reception were efficient and helpful but a smile and a nicer attitude would make me feel more welcome   Small but very comfortable and clean room good location near the tube station Breakfast was on the smaller side but everything was fresh and tasty

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington
Full_Review: Where the toilet is situated in the room was strange Other than that there was nothing at all to complain about As a frequent world traveler and an even frequent traveler to London this has been THE best value for money I have encountered so far   The hotel is modern and the rooms have been updated to modern standards All fixtures and furniture are new Each room has it s own digital heat control The location was very convenient in Earl s Court Lot s of restaurants and 3 tube connections High Street Kensington is a short and beautiful 5 10 minute walk away and the surrounding streets are filled with gorgeous London architecture

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington
Full_Review: Great hotel very well located and fantastic value for money When we left the hotel jet lagged and with small children we forgot to clear our safe deposit box The hotel staff were incredibly helpful looking after our valuables contacting us and helping us arrange for them to be couriered to our next destination Cannot speak highly enough of the excellent service

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington
Full_Review: Can be cold as the air con for heating the room can take a while  Love the hot breakfast although u need to be there at times when the fresh cooked food comes out Staff are very helpful and always happy to help

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington
Full_Review: Their attitude awful in dealing with customers specially their management they should be more respectful in a way they talks The hotel is too expensive compare to the size of the room There is no space for a suit case The toilet is small narrow The elevator in the hotel is too small only for one person has space Not advisable at all 0 score deserves   Nothing

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington
Full_Review: It is advertised as a 4 hotel but that s criminal misrepresentation to be honest I was put in a dungeon of a room with a loo squashed against a wall a shower I couldn t stand up in and a tiny single bed The wifi didn t work painfully slow kept cutting out the TV aerial was broken no TV at all not even terrestrial and I couldn t work out how to let the water out of the bath They don t even provide glasses so you can have a glass of water   The receptionist was nice enough location is handy for Earl s Court tube it was clean and the towels were fluffy that s about it I m afraid

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington
Full_Review: Although the buffet breakfast was good the cooked food was lukewarm at best maybe the temperature of the heaters on the unit needs to be cranked up The hotel had free wifi which is excellent but you need to get a new password every 24 hours which is a bit of a hassle On the day of our check out the TV mysteriously stopped working and wouldn t show any channels and the fire alarm went off in our room only I think these are fairly underhand tactics to get you out of your room before the 10am checkout time which is too early in my opinion   The hotel is really close to Earls Court and West Brompton stations and there s lots of shops and restaurants near by if needed The staff were friendly and attentive

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington

Hotel_Name: Best Western The Boltons Hotel London Kensington

Didn t spend enough time the to notice anything bad  Customer service was excellent single room but had move me to a smaller 1 bed room unfortunate but was ok for what I needed gave me money on account so had a nice burger supper on the hotel

Though 4 star hotel and minibar as per desription it is empty there is even no orange juice available   Staff is more than friendly very very dedicated and helpful Breakfast delicious indeed

Although the hotel was lovely and in good location it is still quite expensive 200 a night   We were greeted well and offered a smaller room that was ready early or the original room a little later and also given complimentary breakfast if we chose the smaller room The water in reception was fab as were the sweets The breakfast was lovely There was banging in the room as a result of improvement work Reception were helpful about time this and reassured us that this would stop by 4pm
Aircon was very noisy Tea coffee etc provided but no cups to drink it from View of brick wall not very appealing   Breakfast very good Staff very friendly and helpful Great location

Room 120 the bathroom of the room above got flooded and the water dripped down through the spotlight fitting into our bathroom However the staff were very quick at resolving the issue and couldn t have been more helpful   The breakfast was excellent the room comfy and the staff friendly and attentive
Smallest hotel room I ve ever stayed in No window view brick wall right outside so limited natural light   Great location good breakfast buffet friendly efficient staff

Shame that the lift is so small and is used by the housekeeping staff but at least there is one We do appreciate that this is because of the constraints of older buildings   Fantastic location with friendly and helpful staff Happy for us to leave our cases on arrival as the rooms weren t ready these were in our rooms for us on our return to the hotel My son appreciated the sweets in reception

Double bed was slightly small but room was quite small too but extremely clean Looked out onto brick wall which made room quite dark would have been nice to have a window view  Lunch in the restaurant was excellent service very good Came back after concert in Hyde park and restaurant about to close but still served us and staff very helpful Breakfast very good food freshly cooked

There s a single much used lift to service the 4 floors of rooms and which is also used by housekeeping and room service so you can feel stuck on the fourth floor Bed was on the very firm side though not uncomfortable A single room is small enough there s no room for a sitting chair besides the one at the desk   The location is near the Bond Street Baker Street and Marble Arch tube stations so easy to shoot off in any directions The included breakfast offers both a cold buffet and choice of hot dishes as well as hot drinks juices If you like to shop Selfridges is nearby as well any number of shops

Not enough electric sockets in room Three in total but two were very high up wall and with no shelf underneath I couldn t plug anything into them So just the one socket which could be used Bit of a nuisance when trying to charge phone laptop etc   Staff were exceedingly helpful and friendly Location excellent Lovely breakfast

Loved the staff as they were ready to answer any questions and the breakfast was fantastic I was traveling alone and the location gave me great access to buses and tubes Would love to stay there again

I can t think of anything negative  Unfortunately i arrived quite late and had an appointment the following morning so i did not get chance to eat breakfast so i can not comment on that The bed was very comfy and the a c was particularly welcome due to the heatwave The room had everything i needed The gentleman on the desk when i checked in was particularly helpful also the following morning when he had already printed directions for me i thought that was fab so thank you to him sorry i didn t get his name I hope to stay again and and spend a little more time in the room and the restaurant next time

Not a first world problem but the bathroom didn t have a lot of space for my toiletires and some were on the floor For a couple the shower is a little small to fit both of you   The location was perfect close to Marble Arch underground buses or walking distance to all the major attractions A big old building so the room was really quiet and the bed and bedding was super comfortable The staff were excellent and very helpful in every way and the buffet breakfast was truly on point A variety of everything for all tastes and the chicken sausages were awesome I had a pair of extremely muddy boots from being at a festival and the hotel gave them a complimentary clean and shine I travel extensively and was really impressed with service of this place

See above   Lovely helpful staff Very quiet location but also convenient for Oxford Street etc Had a really good nights sleep The choice at breakfast was excellent but no pre warmed plates available for hot food which as a result went cold very quickly Scrambled egg too sloppy for my personal taste but you can t please all of the people all of the time WILL BE STAYING AGAIN
The basic room is very very tight However they quite promptly changed us to a better room due to some misunderstanding of what room we were to get   Did not try it
I booked Standard Single room Bathroom was a little small but all toiletries were there   Excellent and safe location Stuff was wery helpful Very good breakfast

Small room  Great location only 300 meter from the famous shop in Oxford street Staff are very nice helpful 1st night after 17 hours flight I got room was very small But after 10 minutes in room suddenly the staff call me and asked about the room I have informed him about the room was too small He promised to change with the bigger tomorrow morning Overall is very good Also near from marble arc station also Bus City Tour stop area

Was put in room 117 really small room Only downside to a decent stay   Fantastic breakfast following day good selection well presented Wi Fi was good speed around 8mb anytime I used it Location was excellent 5 minute walk to Marble Arch tube 70 metres off of Oxford Street
Nothing   The staff here are so accommodating and genuinely nice I never felt that any of my requests and I had many were too much trouble for them They were beyond helpful

Room size bathroom size internet connection the view My room was definitely to small even for one person with two it would be like on submarine Together with terrible view from the window it is difficult to understand price and 4 stars rating   Hotel very was close to my destination The equipment interior and stuff

the room was very very small the shower was very tight the first night was terrible the room 403 was very small and L couldn t open my bag but they changed the room next day the wasn t isolated annoying sound coming from the corridor and the cars outside   location staff

I booked couple of weeks before travelling and I ended up in a microscopic room that was probably no more than 7 m2 Quite frankly for 200 GPB per night the room I got was ridiculous and I say it having been in London many times for business in this category of hotel It was only there for one night but I was annoyed nonetheless as I purposely avoided other less expensive hotels exactly not to end up sleeping in a cage I made my point to the reception at check out and they kindly offered to do better next time which is of course nice but maybe it would have been more appropriate to warn me upon arrival and maybe offer a discount at the point I was asked to pay at check in since I can t possibly believe the one I had was a standard room for single occupancy   Very nice breakfast nice geographical location the hotel was proper and clean

The room and washroom were claustrophobic a lot of noise from lift and people passing the rooms and the curtains were not proper to hide the lights   staffs were helpful and polite and the breakfast was good

The bed and the rooms were awfully small especially the one with the stairs it was ridiculous the showers in the bathroom were too short we had to bend down to wash our hair we could listen to everything outside of the room there arent any mirrors in the room not even slippers and the cleaning lady took a white dress my wife left on the chair mistaking it for towels and when i called they said they ll look for it and get back to me and that was it no feedback and i didnt get it back there was one elevator for service and customers and it was so slow we had to wait for it to come back empty so we could go up or down and the roof of the room was so short if i lift my hand i could touch the wall and i know there are many guys taller than me i think they should crawl around the room l the bed was small for 2 ppl and not comfortable and thats after we changed our room 2 times to find something habitable oh and dont bother spraying perfume youll get to the lobby and start smelling like lobster after that  The staff was friendly

The windows are poorly insulated so the room is cold and you can hear the noise from the street My room was in front of the elevator so again not the ideal location for a good night sleep   Staff were very friendly and helpful

Small room bathroom had no place to hang towel Large step up to bathroom made it difficult to manage Difficult step into shower too Bathroom and room over looked by offices no net curtains so they could see in unless everything closed up  Location

The room itself was very small to share with two single beds There wasn t a lot of room for our luggage   The breakfast in the restaurant was fantastic and the staff were very friendly and helpful The location was great for shops and easy to access the tube for the rest of the city
Convenient location with good public transport Twin bedded room size a little small cannot open 2 luggages at the same time Hotel staff from reception to restaurant are very friendly and helpful
Price of the bar 4 drinks 30   We travelled as a family of 4 adults Nice location easy to get to from tube reception staff really friendly complimentary breakfast was a nice touch and really nice
The room was a little bit small   Radisson Blu Edwardian Sussex has good location for both business and leisure Although the room was not large the facilities and service were superb

Personally I believe that the room price can slightly decrease due to its room size   Radisson Blu Edwardian Sussex is a superb hotel Although its room is not that large its location was perfect Tube and shopping street All staffs have service mind thus Its service was exceed our expectation
Being sent somewhere else because of problems with reservation system despite having had an email from the hotel at 4pm   Did not see enough to form an opinion only got as far as reception
Nothing I didn t like no view from room but that wasn t important as only there to sleep  It was a comfortable room in an excellent location for Oxford street and central London
The room was too warm Bathroom too small not enough room to swing a cat  The fresh fruit was beautiful just ripe enough The sausages were very tasty Staff extremely helpful
I couldn t have breakfast as it only starts at 7 and I was out by 6 30  I arrived to smiles at reception and they could not of been more accommodating

Tiny rooms with low ceilings and bathrooms that barely fit an adult Housekeeping staff incredibly loud every morning they would talk at the top of their voices waking up everyone in the hotel   Great location

Nothing I would stay here again   Amazing location lovely decor Made most of the space for very small rooms The staff was friendly helpful and went out of their way to make sure we were comfortable Breakfast was delicious with great selection

It was the smallest room I have stayed in for a very long time Tiny really The foyer was also very small  Breakfast in the restaurant next door was pretty good The quality of the decor was to a very high Online check in was excellent

Bathroom tiny in room 214 Only a few tea bags in room A few biscuits would have bee a nice touch No lounge area to sit and have a drink Lots of doors banging and noise throughout the night  good location

Tap water running slow just trickling down room 404 Tap in a poor condition Outdated and very heavy chairs in the room Harsh lights in the room   Very professional helpful and cheerful staff Great location Restaurant within the facility For me it was great value for money I was out during the days

Windows not being able to open   The rooms were comfortable but windows being able to open would have added to comfort Staff were amazing courteous helpful friendly I could not find fault Rooms were small but not too bad Well no 24 wasn t Convenient
Bed was not comfortable had sunk on one side Shower gel dispenser did not work so couldn t use the shower gel to shower Eggs a breakfast were a little sloppy   Location was good

On arrival I was told there was nothing available in the room category that I had booked and that I would be charged an extra 50 pounds for an upgrade Unbelievable I refused and they found another room for me   Overall quiet and cozy Fabulous breakfast Great shower but tiny bathroom Nice building and room but no view whatsoever Very convenient location
I requested a twin room However it was not prepared I was upgraded with an additional fee 50 Such a service for the first time Incredible   Location

Extremely slow one elevator water pressure of the shower   We upgraded at the business room wich was recommended from the front disk lady nice room and it comes with breakfast definitely staying again because of the location

A very small room selling a single room as a double Shower room so small it was difficult not to dunk the towel in the toilet when drying Bed falling apart and the frame bigger than the mattress Overall very disappointing and definitely not value for money

Room was shubby Very very small bathroom difficult to operate there even no electric socket for a hsir dryer there the hairdryer itself was hidden in a drawer of a work desk the panel with electrical sockrts on a table was falling away from the table broken latch on the door Very small and uncomfortsble elevator   Location and price
Excellent location to Oxford St Room was comfortable staff were helpful and friendly and went above and beyond to help make my friend s birthday special I would happily stay here when in London again

No mirror in the main bedroom area for females when drying their hair No magnifying mirror in the bathroom Greatly require for people who wear glasses  Great location Good breakfast Room tastefully decorated and warm Staff very friendly throughout hotel

The walls are super thin could hear the carts cleaning staff and tv from next room like it was in my room The rooms were very hot and I could not adjust the heating properly Same for the water in the shower it was very difficult to get the exact temperature   The staff were super friendly and helpful especially at the reception Location was excellent very central but on a quiet side street
There were disgusting curly hairs in the shower The pipes were SO noisy in our room it kept us awake Carpets and fittings really worn and grubby   The staff were really friendly

The rooms are really small and the bathrooms even smaller Had to change rooms as we had booked a twin and were allocated a double Be careful when you book and be very specific  Perfect location just off Oxford Street

The cleaner dosent clean the coffee cups And every day they take the cups and i have to order them the elevator take long time i think 2 would be enough  Staff is very friendly specially Caroline she really helped me for an early check in

The room was small and the bathroom was minute A bedside lamp would have been easier on the eyes than just ceiling spotlights There was low level noise outside the room probably coming from the air conditioning unit take earplugs if you re a light sleeper   The location was good for Oxford Street and Hyde Park The choice at breakfast was good and more varied than other hotels of a similar standard
Minor niggle no electrical sockets by the bed and aircon heating could not change the temperature  Good location for our needs Bed comfortable Staff courteous and helpful

No isolation at the windows No heating in the bathroom freezing cold The AC system was making noise in the middle of the night even when switched off Definitely not a 4 stars as announced Far too expensive for the provided experience  Location Extra comfy beds Friendly staff
Cleanness The bed sheet is dirty in first room with short hair I found and black spots I ask to change a clean bed sheet then they change my room I am not happy with this   Location

Narrow corridors and a somewhat cramped feel   Couldn t get a better location The room is on the smaller side but well maintained and the staff are very friendly and helpful Housekeeping does the bed again the evening
Staff went that extra mile to make our stay as comfortable as possible especially Sinita so big thanks Excellent location Will deffinately return and would highly recommend

Very small rooms I changed 3 rooms because of a noise of the air condition In all 3 rooms the noice was the same I have been told that this is normal and they could not fix it The sound was like bubbles If the air condition was switched off the sound stopped for a while and then it started again Very bad for such a hotel   Top location breakfast very good

It may just be the room I had but the room was very cold when entering the heating system didn t seem to be working properly The wooden trim round the mirror on cupboard was broken meaning the light inside cupboard shone through the gap and I had to stick the switch in place to turn the light off The bed throw and pillow were heavily stained and marked   Hotel reception Restuarant and facilities were of a very high standard I ate in the Restuarant on arrival and the food and service was very good The breakfast buffet was very good and plentiful with and everything and more you ask for to start the day Bathroom amenities were very nice and clean

I would prefer the pillows to be lower but this is quite personal   Overall I enjoyed my stay at the hotel The room is a bit small but contains all the facilities I need Good breakfast and helpful staff

I didn t think the cooked breakfast was fantastic especially for a vegetarian I e BBQ beans service very poor by not replacing plates and cutlery when taken away never got asked if we would like more tea coffee Our room was next to noisy generators and in the morning you could smell the cooked breakfast through the air conditioning the pillows were like rocks and very uncomfortable and the bar did not stay open after 11pm which I thought was poor as we were looking forward to having as drink at the hotel   The room was clean

The price excludes breakfast and the price for adding breakfast was too expensive for my opinion  The location was perfect for walk tours and to the underground All stuff helped us with bright faces and useful tips
My room as the noise of A c was too disturbing and could not let me sleep at peace   The staff as the reception Extremly Service And very helpful and caring

The rooms very warm Also when checking out at 9 45 trying to get from 3rd floor to ground took about 10 mins as lift always full and only one and also used by service users as well We were 15 mins late for an appointment One lift we did not feel was enough especially at busy times Very frustrating  Location and setvice

The room allocated had twin beds which we didn t want so they moved us to a deluxe room for the second night with a double bed There was no tea or cups in the room so had to call house keeping at 7 30a m to get some The bathrooms are very small The room was very hot   The staff were very friendly and helpful The location was great for shopping in Oxford Street

A window blind that allows light e g venetian or net People in the office opposite would be able to see into the room so I had to lower the blind and put the lights on even in daylight   Staff were particularity helpful

The lift is incredibly slow Fortunately being on the second floor I could easily use the stairs   Warm welcome and my room ready early which got my stay off to a grea start Room good size despite being booked as a single Quiet and well equipped

We checked in we were given a room in the gatehouse section of the hotel Now I know the hotel was not busy it was boxing day and only one other guests car in the car park Yet we were dumped in the cold damp section of the hotel probably best only used for the busy summer tourist season The wallpaper was splitting at the seams and peeling from the wall there were cracks all over the ceiling the bathroom although recently refitted was obviously done by cheap unskilled labour I could press the large tiles under the mirror and they would bow and flex into the void behind them Though I did not do this too much as I feared the large mirror would come crashing down The grouting was terrible too or even completely missing The small wooden threshold bordering the bedroom carpet to the bathroom tiles was bowed so every time I stood on it it would flatten down leaving the nail head to stick up very nice for when you walk into the bathroom barefooted But most of all it was cold and damp We stayed in the room an hour and a half but we could not get heat into the room and were not only not satisfied I was annoyed at being mugged off I complained and one of the ladies on the desk seemed a little snobby to start with though she did show us two rooms inside the main building and eventually gave us a free upgrade to a superior room Which was warm and comfortable and well decorated But why do hotel do this I thought it was just me but the more people I talk to this seems common practice to give out the crappy rooms first The second lady on reception even admitted she did not like the gatehouse room when she helped us with our luggage yet she was the one who had initially booked us in and chose the room I have a rule now If I have to complain about the room I never use the hotel again The other thing though minor compared to the room is they have no hot breakfast just a continental breakfast   The stay at this hotel would have been nice if they had simply given us a decent room in the first place But they didn t so I am struggling to find something about this hotel that I really did like

t wasn t until I got home that I realised that I had booked a Queen Room and it was quite clear that the room was a standard double in fact compared to the photos advertised it felt like we d been given the smallest room available The walls are very thin and people walking the corridors at night and early morning could be easily heard Overall I have been left rather disappointed by the experience   We visited this hotel whilst visiting London for a birthday We arrived in plenty of time and were able to check in early approx 11am as the room was ready The staff were pleasant enough and showed us the room The rooms are clean and the bathroom was good The next day they were kind enough to let me leave my car in the car park whilst we went into the city

Check in was quite poor I modified my booking via Bookings com at least 3 months prior to arrival however the hotel had no record of these changes This is the second time that I have stayed at the Pillar Hotel and the reception staff tried to indicate that the previous booking from 2014 was the same as the 2016 booking which wasn t even in the same name It took far too long for reception to sort out whatever needed to be sorted out at their end before providing the room key Also as with the previous booking we had to ask to change rooms after only a few days The rooms don t appear to be maintained as expected as the bathroom tiles were cracked and the floor moved thus creating a health problem as you could not step out of the shower with bare feet We were offered the room next door which had the same issue although to a lesser extent The room that we moved to did not have a working safe and maintenance was not available until the next day Every morning we had to call reception for amenities whether it be towels toilet paper tissues etc as housekeeping didn t replace these as expected Also every day the shower mat was placed inside the wet shower making it sopping wet and unusable There was no business centre with a computer and printing facility that could be used by guests and the staff were very inquisitive as to why such services were needed which frankly is none of their business They tried to assist in locating an internet cafe but where they sent me did not exist As one of the few decent hotels in the area it would be expected that a corner of the hotel should be converted into a business centre to allow guests to have access to necessary services that cannot be accessed via tablets   There was plenty of parking on site Location is easily accessible via road and public transport

I was surprised to see that preauthorisation was not released on my checkout date when the payment was executed but 5 days later I contacted by phone the guest relations manager who blamed the bank for the delay but the truth was that the hotel had given the release order 5 days after the checkout date as being informed by the bank If this is the policy of the hotel it would be better for the client to be aware of it and not be misleaded I sent an urgent email to the manager about this issue and he replied after 8 days Very disappointed by their professionalism  Very good location comfortable rooms nice restaurant and friendly staff
The beds were very small but comfortable never the less Both bars were closed at 10 30pm on Sunday night   The staff were all very helpful and friendly the breakfast room was nice

Hotel_Name: H tel Les Dames du Panth on
Full_Review: This gorgeous little hotel is situated right next to the Pantheon but feels like a quiet respite from the busy streets of Paris From the moment you step in the door you can tell that much care has been taken in decorating and furnishing this hotel There are so many lovely touches throughout the common areas and the guest rooms From the beamed ceilings to the rotary phones and other decor our rooms were beautiful and charming The bathrooms are very modern super clean and comfortable The bed was wonderful And we appreciated the air conditioning Breakfast served in the stone walled cellar was delightful Truly the best scrambled eggs I have ever had The hotel backs up to a quiet neighborhood with a number of restaurants cafes and patisseries that we really enjoyed Last but not least the staff at reception and throughout the hotel were so gracious and friendly We look forward to returning to the Hotel Les Dames du Pantheon

Hotel_Name: H tel Les Dames du Panth on
Full_Review: I got room 1 which is literally by the bar on the ground floor and with room window on the street So obviously noisy and a bit odd Avoid   Splendid location

Hotel_Name: H tel Les Dames du Panth on
Full_Review: I think it would be a good idea to have breakfast as part of the accommodation with a couple tables and chairs outside the hotel just because it is such a perfect location   The location was amazing The staff were welcoming had no difficulty with English and the staff went above and beyond to make sure we had everything and anything we needed

Hotel_Name: H tel Les Dames du Panth on
Full_Review: Small rooms as usual in Paris   Awesomely located you can see the Pantheon form your window Rooms not that big but incredible decoration will repeat if we have the chance

Hotel_Name: H tel Les Dames du Panth on
Full_Review: The hotel was rather expensive and breakfast was not included in the price They said afterwards that the breakfast costs 18 Euros per person There was an extra charge for the city tax Although it claimed to be 4 star it did not have facilities like sauna or gym The safety box was very small and you cannot put your laptop in it

Hotel_Name: H tel Les Dames du Panth on
Full_Review: I didn t try breakfast so can t comment   Great location very easy to get to from airport Lots of little restaurants nearby Pantheon just opposite Lovely

Hotel_Name: H tel Les Dames du Panth on
Full_Review: I think there must be a kettle in the room so a guest can make himself a cup of tea or coffee I was very surprised that we didn t have it considering the fact that this is four star hotel   The staff was very friendly and helpful The room is beautiful and clean the location is perfect

Hotel_Name: H tel Les Dames du Panth on
Full_Review: Our cab ride to the airport left us at the wrong terminal at considerable inconvenience   Small comfortable hotel near the Pantheon but not near most Left Bank sites

Hotel_Name: H tel Les Dames du Panth on
Full_Review: Room was very small and view onto a back courtyard and seemed expensive for what it was   Great location near the Pantheon and close to Luxembourg fast RER tube line to and from Gare du Nord Great breakfast

Hotel_Name: H tel Les Dames du Panth on
Full_Review: Considering the price the room and toilet was small the wifi is slow and definitely the breakfast needs improving   Good location and staff are friendly and helpful they even let us leave our luggage for a few days

Hotel_Name: H tel Les Dames du Panth on
Full_Review: I found my room was very poorly distributed in terms of space It becomes very uncomfortable A door impossible to open and which closes automatically behind you divides the bedroom from the entrance and bathroom toilet area You have to cope with that door several times a day Bathroom and toilet are very oppressive Breakfast is 18 and certainly not worth it Apart from these two points I d say this is a nice hotel   This hotel couldn t be better placed The Panth on is so close you get the feeling you can touch it Charming views

Hotel_Name: H tel Les Dames du Panth on
Full_Review: Staff was less helpful than I have encountered in other Parisian hotels We didn t ask for help though and tend to keep to ourselves so it may be different if you approach them more Even leaving at 5 AM there was no offer to help us with airport transportation The bar set up is weird Not sure how you d even get a drink but it didn t matter because so many things were nearby The pillows were beyond flat this is probably my only real complaint and one night the street was extremely noisy con of a popular area   Location to RER notre dame and cafes The view was great of the Pantheon Luxe decor in a great way All the wall paper was fabric and really made the space romantic and unique The hotel was very clean and you can tell they take care of the space Room size was adequate it is Europe Also they really maximized space with a different layout Good wi fi Overall this was a solid hotel choice with nice amenities and location It definitely has more of a boutique feel than other hotels we ve stayed

Hotel_Name: H tel Les Dames du Panth on
Full_Review: The location of the property was excellent just a short walk from Luxembourg train station with direct links to the airport and walking distance to the major attractions

Hotel_Name: H tel Les Dames du Panth on
Full_Review: Great view of the Pantheon sumptuous interior decor and very friendly staff Within easy walking distance of the RER and metro and in proximity to lots of bars and restaurants

Full_Review: Well as I had a room at the front if you could organise for the local rubbish lorry to either pick up later or do it more quietly that would be amazing It woke me up very early each morning with all the banging and crashing Not the hotel s fault but it was a tad annoying I will stay at the hotel again as very good but will request a room at the back and hope that is quieter   very clean facilities particularly the bathroom Lovely

The only concern were the toilets It was freezing cold and not enough ventilation   I liked the location and cleanliness of the hotel The room was pretty good for a group of three extra bed didn t cause disruption and the staff were very friendly and spoke excellent English

The room doors were LOUD they would slam shut so every time someone left came back to their room you could hear it The walls indoors were pretty thin so you could hear everything going on although I couldn t hear anything from the outside Also I was here on business and the wi fi didn t work from my room It was extremely frustrating The only place it worked was on the bottom floor I think they only had one router for the entire hotel I would reconsider staying here for business again The breakfast was okay but in my opinion it s better to go to one of the delicious bakeries or creperies down the street   The location of the hotel was amazing close to everything While the room felt pretty small the view from the balcony was nice the heater worked well and the bed was very comfy They also have a spa downstairs which you can reserve for your private use for an hour small gym electronic massage chair and a hammam

Its the best hotel ever Really there is no way to have as this comfort cleanliness I wont tell you about the staff They are so gentel as our family I will stay in hotel grand saint michel when i will come to paris Its the best of the best I adore

The room was dusty yet clean enough to stay   Breakfast was good A C was also one of the best I have ever seen slient and fast warming Hotel is close enough to Pantheon walking distance to RER B and other metro lines Staff was helpful Itineries were good

Having to leave to come home   Love everything about this hotel Fabulous room super comfortable massive bed Everywhere clean fresh soft towels The area feels safe good selection of bars and restaurants Close to metro short walk to Pantheon and Notre Dam Staff are friendly welcoming and helpful During our stay it was my birthday and on retiring to our room I found a bowl of fruit nuts and cakes and a message from the staff I was so pleased We used the hotel shuttle to travel to the airport and the driver came back to the airport to pick us up after we spent 90 minutes getting through border control Couldn t fault it Will return to this hotel every time in Paris b
Breakfast was fantastic I also thought it was the perfect location for Paris with walking distance to most everything and easy access to Metro Super convenient from airport

Pillows were too hard and bulky to our taste Internet was working badly It comes and goes often They have two networks but none of them was working well One evening we were trying to watch something online for 40 mins and then we just gave up it was not working at all So it would be great to improve Internet   We really liked following things 1 Location it is very close to metro and very close to many places to see In fact we walked to all places we wanted to see except Monmartre where we took metro It is also not on very noisy and crowded street For us it was very important as some streets in central part of Paris are so crowded and noisy that it is not pleasant for stay Surrounding was also clean and more tidy than some other places in Paris 2 Breakfast was very nice It was much better than in many hotels I have seen Many different options for food They had afternoon tea with sweets which was also very nice experience 3 It has steam bath hamam and a small gym We have not used gym but we used hamam couple of times It was great After long walks in the city it was wonderful to get soaked and relax in the evening You just need to book in advance with the reception it is free 4 Bathroom was very nicely equipped If you could have forgotten anything everything was provided

Would be nice if they offered full bar service in the area off of the lobby in the late afternoon early evening   Love the location staff and general layout of the rooms on quiet street Very clean

Nothing   This was beyond wonderful There is no need to search any longer for the best Paris Hotel Beyond the fantastic location which is right near pont du neuf set on a quiet street off an alley way that was safe 1 2 a block to the metro across from the Louvre and many restaurants within feet of you what makes the hotel literally the very best we have stayed at through all our Europe traveling from London to Paris to Rome is the staff Frederique and the rest of the staff were all so caring patient sweet and above all knowledgable Anything we needed from a taxi restaurant laundry exceptional and always strong wifi food drinks help and anything else especially being new to the area and traveling with kids from ages 6 thru 15 they provided Each day 24 hours someone was there at the desk in case you needed anything at all or had any questions We are so grateful to them all for making our stay so comfortable The breakfast served delivered daily to your room was plentiful delicious very reasonable for the amount of food they provided and exceptionally delicious Actually it was the best chocolat croissants and coffee we had the entire trip The room was the adjoining two rooms for a large family of 5 or more It was a great size two bathrooms all you needed in the room including TV a mini bar safe that only you have the key too and the most comfortable beds We had 4 beds for 5 people Each Kid had their own area and we had our own area as well Yes one shower was slightly small but the other made up for it so no one had an issue They had a lift if needed The wifi was always working and always strong as was the air conditioning The atmosphere was perfect for all ages What else can I say about how amazingly great the hotel location and staff were Basically we will never stay anywhere else when visiting Paris This will be it We thank them all and miss them already

The only rooms with two beds are right by the lift so we had a lot of noise from returning Royal Albert concert goers   The perfect floor plan and well thought out storage made our small room feel spacious and easy to keep pretty looking The huge light bathroom was glorious after all the dangerous cramped facilities in our other hotels All meals we ate in the restaurant were as pretty to look at as they were delicious We had the duck mousse with rhubarb jam twice

The Gore has a great potential The locations is steps away from Hyde Park Royal Albert Hall and the Museums Unfortunately at the time of my visit the facade was under renovations with scaffold all the way covering the windows without letting me know There was either no privacy or I had to keep the windows closed and covered The pluming needs work the shower had either cold or hot water but not the mixed one It made is impossible to use it The sink did not drain properly Small things but makes the difference The biggest problem came at dinner time They not only hurry us up we dine at 10 after the Prom concerts and they knew it but I was also overcharged and had to dispute the bill the next day Quality of food was good but too bad if you finish the dinner like this The room was good nice size good quality but on the top floor and access was difficult no elevator However the staff was friendly and made the luggage trip easier I wish I did not get disappointed by the dinner bill and by the fact that somehow a hotel should inform upfront its clients if construction works are being done during the booking

No fault of the hotel but the building exterior is currently surrounded by scaffolding so could get noisy as work is being done Hopefully the hotel will look fresh and new once the work is all complete   The room was cosy with lots of period features and extravagant furnishings Booked this hotel as it was near to Hyde park and also around the corner from the Albert Hall

It was under renovation so after 8 30am it was a bit loud and we needed to keep the curtains shut Wifi was very slow I resorted to my LTE connection over the phone   Amazing place Amazing location and very nice staff Already booked it again

The room wasn t ready although we arrived half an hour after the designated check in time we were on a strict time schedule so this was unhelpful to say the least as we wanted to change and freshen up before going out again and we were encouraged to have a drink in the bar while we waited at our own not inconsiderable expense The room was very small and much of the natural light was blocked out by the extensive scaffolding that covered the entire building In addition to boil the kettle it was necessary to plug it in almost at floor level as the waist high sockets were all non compatible continental style The wi fi is hit and miss   The bed was extremely comfortable The concierge was very friendly and the common interior parts of the hotel are beautiful Breakfast little more than adequate

Way too expensive  Unfortunately the lovely pictures of the property raised my expectations Musty and smelly Poor staff w poor English speaking skills Unable to have the Room I actually booked Seriously overpriced

Most hotels hotels at this price range offer free water wider range of teas biscuits etc A very minor gripe but it seems a little stingy   Amazing history 1890 s hotel with well designed decor to fit the period Beggars banquet launch party was held here Convenient for Albert hall Fabulous oak bedstead looked like early Liberty gothic arts and crafts
The water tempetature was a little difficult to get right   The staff were excellent Our room was nice and the bed quite comfortable It was lovely and quiet on the top floor

The first room we were given was very badly layed out Having had a busy day we wanted to spend a quiet evening in our room and watch the TV However due to the TV being positioned in a recess we could not see the full screen plus the view was further obstructed by one of the bed posts In fact the only place one could actually view the full screen of the tv was by sitting on the desk which was clearly not acceptable After speaking to reception we were offered an alternative room which was better however the bed was so high my wife had to use a chair to get in it I am almost 6 foot tall and I even found it a struggle to get into bed There was also a dreadfully disturbing repeated sound of running water which appeared to be coming from pipes or drains within wall cavities I contacted the duty manager at 10 30pm and she came to the room and agreed the noise was dreadful The overall experience was certainly not what I would have expected considering the room rate I was paying On checkout we were given a 10 discount off our bill but as our stay was intended to be a treat for my wife s birthday the experience fell well short of our expectations   All the staff tried to be helpful and the location is good

Refrigirator in the room not working no drawer to put underwear and others Personally we found the price of 867 93 to expensive for 3 nights Bathroom very small Walking distance to far from the Tube 1 km   Breakfast and diner very good 190 Bar pleasant employee very kind and helpful

The only thing I didn t like was long life UHT milk to have a tea in your room and a couple more vegetarian options for room service would have been fab   Gorgeous decor the bed was very comfortable and the best part the shower The building had scaffolding up so it took a while to actually find

room small and very dark and looking out of date Also scaffolding outside window so no view was only told this on our way up to the room shame really had expected more   convenient location
