The hotel was undergoing renovations so it was clad in scaffolding However as our stay was over a weekend there was no work going on so it was no problem It was a shame to not be able to take a picture from the outside but we ll be sure to do that next time we come   Right from the moment we walked in the door the staff were amazing attentive polite and gave us the impression that nothing was too much trouble

The lift didn t work until the moment we left The room was boiling hot and the temperature controls didn t work We ordered room service and after a very long wait the meal was delivered incorrectly We asked for semi skimmed milk to be delivered to our room and got full fat Not many people seemed to speak or understand English   We were visiting the Albert Hall This hotel is an excellent spot for access to this venue Staff were attentive and everyone tried hard to make our visit comfortable The room was comfortable and full of interesting furniture and fittings

Tiny room 110 at the front overlooking street so quite noisy Just enough space to squeeze round the bed Even tinier bathroom Snooty foreign reception staff with a really bad attitude  Perfect location for the Royal Albert Hall just round the corner Nice bar

Concierge was particularly helpful Knew all about buses taxis times journeys would take If you like antiques old paintings you will be happy We did not eat in hotel but it looked excellent Small bed room but v comfy

The noise The elephant in the room above wasn t very considerate with regards to their heavy feet on the old floorboards at 2am in the morning If you re a light sleeper take earplugs as the doors don t close gently either   Beautiful hotel in a fantastic location was ideal for the purpose of our trip for a concert at the RAH Extremely comfy bed and the staff were friendly and accommodating

Nothing Loved it and would stay here again   Very nice comfortable hotel Lovely fixtures and fittings and the staff were excellent too Close to the Royal Albert Hall and about a ten minute walk to the closest tube station We had a four poster bed which needed a ladder to climb on to It was very comfortable though

Zip Zilch Nada   The feeling that you re staying in a glamorous Edwardian private residence The generous sized Penhaligons toiletries the sumptuous bathroom pillow selection Also being able to open the window for fresh air a great bonus
Staff had good humour and extreamly helpful The breakfast from the presentation to the eating delicious Will defiantly be staying again
The room we had this time 102 would not be suitable for people with mobility issues as bed very high and bath hard to get into  Stayed here before lovely hotel with great staff Close to Albert hall

The third bed really just a camp cot was the most uncomfortable bed I have ever slept on A hotel of this caliber could do better than that Or simply state that a second room was their recommendation   The two Concierge were very helpful and delightful to speak to

Me and my partner stayed last night as we were going to the Royal Albert Hall which is a 2 min walk from the hotel to watch Russel Howard Lovely chap on reception when we arrived Showed us to our room Lovely decor all round the hotel which is one of the reasons we booked in the first place Bed was so comfy I m 8 months pregnant so this was fantastic Staff were all polite and friendly We parked at the Horton street car park which was just a 15 20 min walk from the hotel and very reasonable Would definitely recommend this hotel

unfriendly staff To the point of being quite rude dreadful breakfast and service in the breakfast room mice in the Bar staff attitude well below what you would expect for this hotel Scaffolding on front of building workmen able to look right into our room not advised of building work or offered another room very poor indeed   Good location for the Royal Albert Hall Beautiful building and decor

breakfast was very poor for what they charge the hotel is about 10 15 minutes walk to the nearest tube not so convenient if you are on the move all time   the decoration was fine and the neighborhood nice and close to the Natural History Museum and the V A museum there are some fine restaurants around

We ordered room service an item which was priced at 8 on the menu We ordered 2 When the food was delivered wonderful service we were charged 22 50 and we have no idea why   The staff were extremely friendly and approachable the room was gorgeous with plenty of tea and coffee The breakfast was amazing beautifully presented foods at the buffet and I had the Canadian pancakes which were just perfect The tea and coffee is also freshly brewed The bed was very comfy and you could ask for different pillows depending on what type of sleep you wanted

Tea coffee facilities in room very basic I ve stayed in rooms less than third of price with biscuits basket of fruit offered   Beautifully furnished gorgeous large bath friendly staff esp in restaurant Gore 23 breakfast exceptional choice value Loved the brief visit
Away from underground stations   The style of the furniture very large room and bathroom friendly staff quick check in out few steps away from the museums and the Hydepark
Shower dripped but not really a problem   the bed extra comfy partner complained about ours when we came home We d love to know where they got their pillows from

I didn t like the fact that I booked this room back in December 2016 and stated clearly that it was for 2 adults and a young child I was told 1 day before we travelled that there were no rooms left with a double and single bed and that a childs cot would not fit in the room We were then told we could stay at their sister hotel but would have to pay a further 100 to have a bed for my child in that room Could this not have been said to us when we made the booking so we could organise something or cancel the reservation Why wait till the day before we travel to tell us Not sure who the fault lies with whether it s the hotel or booking com but it wasn t good enough and we won t be using either again   The hotel was in a nice location and the staff were nice

Seats in window just as you walk in bar reserved big table when they arrived could not get through as the large party in the way of doorway perhaps they should have been seated elsewhere not to obstruct the door  Very friendly met at door bags carried for me with such a friendly smile

Bed was a bit hard uneven Final account per room night was different to the confirmation from booking com I have queried this with the hotel and am awaiting feedback Hot water for shower taps was a bit flaky in Room 504 it eventually got hot but took a while   The location in Kensington is excellent This is a traditional British hotel in decor atmosphere and some quirkiness Great to have Bar 190 next door with the history of The Rolling Stones album launch in1968 Room was a generous size for London the Penhalion toiletries were my favourite and a decent hairdryer Overally a lovely room in a lovely hotel and good value for money
The buffet breakfast was very limited but very good quality There was the option of cooked breakfast and other hot meals but you had to pay extra   The staff were very welcoming
Tv very small not located in front of bed but rather on the side Service in breakfast room not very attentive Rooms very small No space to put luggage   Friendly staff Gods area Quiet

Our room was very small and the bathroom obviously very close to the bedroom hence very little privacy The bathroom did not have any extraction fan so the steam filled the room very quickly to the point of the walls dripping The shower was ornate but literally flooded the bathroom as there was nothing to trap the spraying shower water We had to use the bathrobes on the floor to soak up the water   Beautiful old property location to the Royal Albert Hall was excellent Nice bar

Smart and stylish This hotel is a gem beautiful surroundings full of antiques polished floors chandeliers and superb manners from all staff English elegance at its best The room was large the bed was very comfortable and at night with the windows open despite the fact that the room faced onto Queen s Gate there was no noise I hope to return
Would have been nice to have a tea and coffee tray in the room only negative   The rooms were incredible couldn t have wished for a better stay Staff were lovely and very helpful
A little too keen to clean the room on the morning of departure although they apologised for intruding too early   Very comfortable bed and room Wonderful service from all the staff

Perhaps tea and coffee with the kettle in the room would have been nice but this is not a major complaint only a passing thought   The staff were particularly charming and most especially Trevor who made us feel very very much at home We shall definitely visit this hotel next time we come to London
No tea coffee facilities in room little storage and only one small chair for what was supposed to be a double room i e catering for two people  Room ok clean and quiet but very small
Not a lot Pretty well on the button   Excellent location for the Royal Albert Hall First class staff who were most helpful especially Trevor at the front desk Good room at a fair price

The room we were allocated been coming to this hotel for over 2 decades but when I am leaning over the bath to get hot water to wash my hands because it takes so long for hot water to reach tap in basin it s time to quit coming here beyond quirky now tired   The other rooms

The only fault I found was although there was three staff on with only four tables to cover at the time they were a bit slow two of them were busy chatting to one another   We loved the stay at the Gore from the second we walked through the door reception staff wear very helpful The room was beautiful as was the bathroom The only fault was the room was a bit to warm for us this is January but that s us The breakfast was well presented and set out in a very beautiful room
Above all I liked the bed the pillows and the towels The beautiful residential area of South Kensington is quiet but convenient and the proximity to Hyde Park is one of the hotel s assets
Very good service on check in staff were courteous and personable Bed was very comfy Easy walk into Hyde Park and High St Ken The library room is nice

The Gore Hotel was Beautiful the staff were very helpful and friendly It was in a brilliant location close which made our stay a lot easier The staff looked after our bags after we checked out so we could go shopping around London which was great Would highly recommend
amazing staff real english old good style of concierge sorting out all questions and requests Great bar with proper barman and of course library is rad

The rooms are never big enough but it is London   I love the charm of the old building the colors in the rooms the breakfast every morning in the restaurant in the front parlor the bar for a drink or a quick meal all the rooms are very special the Staff was always friendly and helpful and the fact they is a nice small hotel well located

Usually stay in a standard room which we love upgraded this time and was extremely disappointed with the superior room as the standard rooms are much better   The beds are the most comfortable ever and the location is great The bar is also lovely just a tad pricey

I had problems with my internet   Lovely helpful staff especially Thomas the concierge Beautiful decor and situated in lovely quiet and safe part of London Good breakfast and lovely library Would stay again

We would have liked to try the breakfast but we did find that this was the only thing that was rather pricey within the hotel   The location of the hotel for the sights we wanted to see was excellent The staff were extremely courteous friendly and helpful For the location and star rating of the hotel we felt the price we got was good value
Mobile phones allowed in dinning room sat next to visitor who was talking on her phone all through our breakfast   Customer service Good selection breakfast menu

All the Staff were very friendly and always happy to help Concierge escorted us to our lovely Miss Ada suite and explained where everything was and how things worked He also gave us excellent advice regarding the most direct route we could take to our concert venue The room was lovely clean and well supplied with all we needed for our overnight stay We were able to ask for a tray to make tea in our room which was supplied without any difficulty The bed was very comfortable and several little extra touches made our stay even more enjoyable We will definitely visit again

very grubby room dirty stained carpet old dirty light switches noise from upstairs all night and next door rooms no shower curtain or screen in the bathroom broken loo seat broken hot tap  nice friendly staff

The rooms are small but normal for the area and I could hear someone snoring in the next room It s an old building which is charming but I like modern hotels  Perfect location Helpful and friends staff Warm welcome and they brought us tea making facilities on request

published checkin time of 2pm missed by 50 minutes no attempt to apologise or ensure comfort while waiting That matters for an hotel proud to be only 150 yards from Albert Hall but nevertheless leaves us with only minutes to spare for an afternoon event breakfast buffet offering is minimalist compared to other 4 stars staff don t seem committed interested or pro active  Beds v comfy location excellent bar comfortable Cooked breakfast offering is varied and value for money

The staff seemed a little distracted but very helpful once you got their attention I think it was a very busy weekend for them   The room was lovely and quiet and the communal areas were beautiful especially the central staircase and the library The breakfast was one of the best I ve had in a hotel

Music in bar inappropriate for style of hotel Playing modern pop when jazz or classical would be more appropriate We were informed afternoon tea available in library However when requested at 4pm it wasn t available However we were served coffee   Cleanliness Comfort Quietness Good breakfast
However the breakfast is completely over priced We had poached eggs toast x 2 tea a latte for 40 Outrageous   Helpful staff good bar lovely decor great location that s why we keep coming back
Only one very small thing there was no tea or coffee making facilities in the room   Great location for visiting the Albert Hall Friendly helpful staff Very comfy bed Lovely toiletries

No tea or coffee making facilities in the room Had an after show coffee in the bar 5 for an average latte with no biscuit We felt that even for central London this was pushing it a bit   The concierge was very helpful almost seemed to be running reception We were struggling to book a pre theatre meal at the hotel and he offered to assist if we could not get one Also got us a cab he does not use uber but traditional black cabs fab service
We are staying since 12 years regulaty during our business trips at the Gore A very nice place with fantastic beds super good drinks in the Bar and a top breakfast We simply love it
I felt very welcome comfortable and cared for I hoped for a relaxed and special trip away and I m am so pleased to say that was my experience The meal prepared for me was delicious also
Need an extractor or openable window in the bathroom of the room we stayed in   Friendly staff Great location for visiting the Albert Hall or the Natural History Museum V A etc

The price was quite high for a small room and the internet was hopeless   Excellent location polite and helpful staff Great for runners very close to London parks Lovely resident lounge that is comfortable and with an open fire

Plumbing Olde worlde taps didn t provide a very good shower experience Lack of tea and coffee making facilities in bedrooms Limited room service menu   Great for the Royal Albert Hall Very helpful staff

Minor dislike but not a huge deal was the thin walls We were able to hear guests next door leave and arrive But I can understand as this is a period building   I loved everything about this hotel The location is perfect for family with small children You have all the museums on the same block Kensington Grounds is less than 5mins walk You can spend a whole day at Kensington park beautiful walks the children can feed the ducks look around the Kensington Gardens for free and have lunch at the cafe We discovered the Princess Diana memorial park which is just perfect for babies toddlers and children It s not your average park btw There s loads for kids to do So the Gore is a perfect hotel for getting to these places without having to tube The hotel is very clean and decor is beautiful if you like that period type of interior The staff are super friendly and attentive to your needs We stayed on the 4th floor We had our baby and a pram and the lift although small it was good enough for getting us up and down We checked out at 11am but was happy that we were able to have our bags stored downstairs whilst we checked out the natural museum and science museum before heading home Thomas was super kind to let us leave our bags at the hotel storage room Breakfast and dinner here is a must The food taste deliciously amazing Will def be staying here in future

The lack of tea and coffee making facilities in the room   Thomas the concierge was most helpful and a real asset to the hotel Although our room was not particularly spacious it was well furnished and worked well for a short stay

The cleaning The lift was very old and not working properly but as its an old property the lift i guess is all part and parcel but was not helpful when you have had a knee operation   The location was excellent bed very comfortable
No tea coffee making facilities in the room No bottled water   The bed was extremely comfortable Great location Loved the adjoining bar Staff very helpful Would definitely return

Nothing We were so pleased with our stay at the Gore Hotel   It s location to Kensington and knotting hill Every place we wanted to go was walkable The hotel was very charming and the staff very helpful

No green tea with lemon available at breakfast Breakfast limited if you do not eat bread or pastry Line of 4 young men across the front door of the hotel smoking when I came back one evening   It has character and is not standard chain product Staff at reception very helpful

There was not a single thing that I didn t like about this hotel It was superb   Everything about this hotel The decor made me think of a past luxurious age gorgeous antique furniture and decorations throughout the hotel We had a really lovely bedroom The staff were very friendly and welcoming The Concierge and Reception staff couldn t do enough for you the Concierge was very helpful and went out of his way to help us The Reception Staff were very informative We hope to be back at this hotel in the near future We were attending a concert at the Albert Hall and the location was superb we were back in the hotel just after the concert in time to have a nightcap drink which just made the evening complete

Room was not as bright as we d expected and furnishings were a bit tired There was no mirror for hair drying as dryer couldn t be used in the bathroom and there was only a curved mirror at high level in the room One of the female bar staff members was particularly unpleasant about reserving seats in the bar for post theatre drinkers and even though we had spent all evening dining at the restaurant we were told we couldn t sit in the bar at the end of our meal for our digestives even though we were guests General manager apologised and it was resolved but staff should use more courteous ways of approaching that situation  Restaurant was exceptionally good breakfast both mornings was faultless and the evening meal in Sat night was a really nice dining experience The guy at concierge was very helpful with parking the car and sorting out a portable mirror for the room

The full English breakfast was a little disappointing Most of the breakfast apart from the eggs was grilled and not fried so the bacon was hard No kettle in the room so couldn t have tea or coffee in the room in the morning   Requests for firmer pillows and Iron and ironing board were dealt with promptly Staff were friendly A stone s throw from the Royal Albert Hall

Maybe a kettle in the room to make early morning tea or coffee  The breakfast was very good with a buffet and one could choose one hot dish such as any type of cooked egg with bacon tomatoes mushrooms Service was very good and friendly Good central location with easy access to public transport Room and bed comfortable Good restaurant

Rubbish shower in bathroom no wash flannel no tea making facilities in bedroom We were on the 5th floor the lift only goes to 4th the stairs are steep my wife and I were ok with it but others with walking problems would not We were never advised of this possible problem  Location excellent front of house staff

Coffee making facility in the room  The staff are the friendliest and the most helpful I have ever come across in many years of visiting London and in particular the Concierge The rooms are spacious and the beds extremely comfortable and housekeeping very efficient
Nice suite very comfortable bed Great cocktail bar with huge range of drinks and good atmosphere Hotel staff go out of their way to make your stay special Thanks to a great team
No coffee tea tray in bedroom Bedroom carpet was stained in various places  Very nice room Very good pre show dinner Location superb for Royal Albert Hotel
Missed having tea making facilities   Friendly helpful staff Surprisingly quiet for such a good central location Excellent breakfast Comfortable bed Easy walk to the Royal Albert Hall
Non functioning geriatric furniture and a bed which was unbelievably soft and offered no support My back was not in good shape the following day   Staff extremely helpful and friendly
It s quite expensive but beautiful   Super helpful staff from reception to bar service Amazing service Beautiful building this was our 2nd stay and we will be back next Year

The wifi was only good with texting If I wanted to use internet I would have to pay extra   Beautiful classic English hotel with study Very traditional Quality room linens supplies and state of art shower

Not well services by public transport as tube network is not nearby even though location is very central  Staff friendly helpful and providing old world service Hotel is breath of fresh air compared with modern soulless hotels with its old furniture and slightly creaky feel

Room awfully small for a deluxe double Plumbing wasn t great and room had an odd odor because of it Breakfast very over priced   Cozy classic ambience in nice quiet neighbhood Had wonderful dinner in on site restaurant and comfortable adjoining bar was a plus

That they tried to transfer us to the Paleham Hotel which I did not like   The hotel was beautiful I liked the decor just to my taste Fantastic location near Hyde Park Staff was very friendly and was extremly helpful
The staff are all very nice friendly and ready to help Breakfast lunch dinner they serve in the restaurant are superb The location is great

Room Wass fraction small but that is expected in London  What a great find Looked closely at accommodation in London and this hotel was perfect for what we were after historic hotel with lovely English decor friendly staff quality fittings very comfortable bed terrific cocktail bar and room had good views over Kensington rooftops

I didn t notice until after I booked that the hotel is a fair distance from any metro station Also the beds I encountered in London including this hotel are platform beds with hard mattresses   The rooms are lovely and the staff is very helpful to travelers The neighborhood is very safe and fine for walking

The first room we were in there was an issue with the shower The water would not get hot We let the desk know on the first day they had maintenance look at it There was no change the second day we said something to the desk and they upgraded us to a different room The only other issue was that the cleaning woman was very gruff and aggressive towards us because we put the Please Service Room sign up but hadn t left yet   The decor was lovely The front desk staff was very friendly and helpful When we had an issue with our room they transferred us to an upgraded room without us even having to ask and at no additional cost Another plus was that they had an American outlet by the desks in the rooms Very convenient We would definitely stay here again

Nothing   Very comfy bed Bathroom absolutely spotless Good quality cotton bedding Resident s lounge a pleasure to be in with interesting art Three minute walk from Albert Hall Food excellent also service

There was no marmite In an English hotel The staff not being British nationals hadn t heard of it nor the chef Not good enough The breakfast room staff needed more training we had to ask for things which should have been offered eg sauces more tea empty plates tidied away   The concierge was very helpful and friendly Lovely breakfast
Breakfast was a little chaotic coffee cup was dirty Slowish service compared to the bar staff s approach the night before   Great room lovely setting bar staff service perfect

In my parents room no one switched on the air on for them and being reticent they didn t know how to or who to ask they therefore boiled opened the window for air and suffered London noise   Staff v friendly but access to room a bit difficult as lift didn t reach 5th floor

No tea coffee making facilities in the room The insulation between our room and next door was not good unless our neighbours were particularly noisy   Position character charming staff attractive traditional dining room good size bedroom

Initial booking was wrong Didn t seem to know how many people were staying and was offered an alternative hotel at 11pm Train tickets were thrown away by mistake by maid but no help offered when tickets couldn t be replaced   Room decor was done in good taste

Please see above  Staff very friendly and helpful Magdalena Trevor Thomas and Francesca All went out of the way to serve Towels and linens very clean and the room was made up nicely everyday But the bed was too soft and the furnishings were too ancient

Picked this hotel because of the location and it was excellent with lots of local shops and places to eat nearby in the roads away from the main road The staff were very helpful and went out of their way to ensure that we had any information we needed and advise about local amenities The hotel is decorated in a traditional style suitable to the period which is very well done I can see that other reviews have interpreted this d cor as tired but although perhaps there is some wear on the carpets in some places the hotel is very clean and the overall look is well created It s probably best not to stay here if you like white bright minimalist style The bedroom was a little small but adequate and the bed was very comfortable and we had a view of the Royal Albert Hall so very pleasant I would definitely stay here again
Room luxurious in the old Rococco style Wonderful bid comfortable bed Very welcoming helpful staff incredible location opp Hyde Park Home away frm home

It was a bit pricey but that is just London And they were doing some construction during our stay but it was quiet at night   It was one block from our meeting at Imperial Innovations so that was great Would also be perfect for Royal Albert Hall and many other things The concierge was wonderful Great staff all around I d go back

The Wi Fi was a little suspect even if you were prepared to pay for additional band width but this is a minor reservation   It was amazingly quiet which was what I had asked for The location was ideal for the major museums and the Albert Hall and even the newest taxi drivers know 190 Queens Gate

Access wasn t ideal for a pram and I imagine a nightmare for a wheelchair but given the age of the building this is totally understandable Carpets in the room very tired but I have paid more in London for hotels that haven t come close to the Gore   Location staff were very professional and friendly and the hotel has a superb unique character Ideal location for the park and museums which are all within walking distance even with young children

A couple of bizarre things given the cost ot the room 1 There was no tea and coffee provided in the room When we asked it seemed like they just didn t want to provide any You get tea and coffee in a Travelodge room so can t understand why they don t do it many other reviews said the same thing as I found out later 2 It took us a day to get an extra bed side lamp in our room Again very bizzare 3 The room was very nice but the carpet looked quite stained again odd for such an otherwise up market hotel   Location was excellent plus we got an upgraded room Service was good and the bed was very comfortable Air con was a bonus too

I would like tea and coffee in the room because I don t like asking for room service when I m rushing to get ready  The staff truly make this place A warm welcome and nothing is too much trouble Easy check in and out
A few mistakes were made on the final invoice breakfast was ok but a bit overpriced   I liked the overall look and feel The hotel has a nice atmosphere and a great location
The bed was so comfy just what was needed after a day out exploring London The hotel was quiet and very glamorous and the cocktails were amazing

Rooms are small noisy u can hear next door people even if someone walking in the corridor food is not nice hotel is kinda old toilets are very small the room in the booking was saying 21 m2 but it was even less than 13 m2  Staff are friendly
Room service food was terrible stale sandwiches and cold chips Breakfast staff were unfriendly  The bar team were great The rooms were spacious comfy clean

The receptionist said when we arrived that we had been upgraded because of our request for a room with a walk in shower but the room didn t have this so we ended up paying quite a lot extra for a junior suite to get what we wanted The staff seemed a bit confused about which rooms of which grade had which facilities   The extremely comfortable bed with beautiful high quality linen The calm and civilised atmosphere

The doorman porter concierge was just a little too jolly for my liking but others may prefer that At least he was happy and friendly No de caff tea of coffee available in the bar   Fabulously comfortable beds in twin room Room acceptable size considering location

Nothing really but if I was being picky the bar is very expensive but it is central London   This is a charming hotel beautifully decorated and in an amazing location The staff were all very accommodating and pleasant the doormen were especially friendly and helpful and that just always helps to make your day go well I had a classic double room and it was restful and peaceful despite facing the road the traffic is really not a problem I had a wonderful weekend here

1 Classic double room is very tiny suitcases need to be stored under the bed otherwise no room to walk need to move rubbish bin to fully open the door to the small cupboard only drawers available are the drawers in the desk 2 internet very slow or absent   Position Friendly helpful staff Comfortable bed

I thought the decor was a bit dark but that is my personal taste   everyone was very helpful very comfortable and although I did not have the breakfast I think 23 is quite expensive especially if you dont have cooked breakfast Very comfortable bed and windows you can open

See above  Second time we ve stayed and this time room not so good looked a bit tired Staff at bar and reception were excellent especially Trevor Staff at breakfast very pleasant but we had to ask for things which should have been offered eg sauces and also had to ask for them twice However it is a very comfortable relaxing place to stay in a first class location and we ll definitely be back
A little bit far from the underground station   The concierge are very nice and helpful Really like the traditional British style decorations Just a few minutes walk to Hyde Park
There was nothing that I didn t like   Lovely bed and such comfortable pillows Delicious meal from room service So close to the Royal Albert Hall for a show

This was an old country house stay but in London like stepping back in time but panelled bed well appointed room but with great bathroom but no bath just a large shower it was really spacious recently redecorated It was hot outside so the airconditioning was perfect The staff on duty when we arrived at 830 pm were so helpful We parked on a yellow line outside until 0830 Which was perfect for Business trip The bed linen was luxurious and Was very comfortable Overall we would go back

Upgrade might not have been worth it as we didn t see the other rooms  Restaurant breakfast 4 poster bed an decor throughout Staff very friendly Bathroom very nice Clean throughout Best G Ts ever in the bar

Nothing  Gem of a place to stay after the ballet at Albert Hall Such lovely cosy Olde World wood decor Fab club sandwiches in bar after and large choice of drinks too This was our second stay and can t wait for next time Thank you

Nothing   The Hotel staff couldn t do enough for us They went out of their way to make our stay special Due to availability our room was upgraded which was brilliant The Hotel was clean location was good facilities were great I can t rate it enough will definitely be going back

We would have liked tea making facilities in the room   We loved the character of the building and the location for the Royal Albert Hall was ideal The staff were all excellent

Bed a bit high for me but very comfortable  Staff amazing nothing was too much trouble Location perfect Decor wonderful Absolutely wonderful classy hotel will definitely go there again

Room service menu far too limited but breakfast options excellent and made up for it   Wonderful Hotel like stepping back in time helpful staff delightful rooms

Having desturbed night due to air con located on roof However this was attended to immediately I drew attention to it and I was given a different room and upgrade the next night   Peaceful and comfortable place to stay attentive responsive and friendly staff

Their wasnt anything we didnt like  We couldnt not fault anything the room service was quick the breakfast was excellant and the room was beautiful and the bed was very comfortable and the staff treated you as friends not just as a customer and were very helpful

Exemplary Wonderful staff and service This is our second stay and we extended as The Gore is so lovely Nothing is ever a problem All staff go out of their way to make your stay as relaxed and as easy as possible Particular mention to the Reception team who extended our stay without any fuss arranged we stay in the same room and simply made everything so effortless Exemplary hotel
the Wall was quite thin and could hear the next door TV in the middle of the night   Very comfortable bed and great service Staff were so friendly and quick to response
The TV in the room in bad position you should never have to turn sideways to watch TV otherwise excellent  Good location wonderful Bar area staff lovely bar food excellent

Like many people who live in Europe I am always rather disappointed by the fact that buffet breakfast is not included in the price in Europe particularly Austria it is always included London is very expensive especially for those who live in Euros   The buffet breakfast was fine and the dining room and bar area very pleasant Also the staff were very helpful efficient and friendly

Lack of drawer space in the bedroom For one night it did not matter but for a longer stay would not want to keep non hangables in my suitcase   Easy walking distance to the Royal Albert Hall Polite courteous staff
no tea coffee facility in room nor any complementary bottled water tap water foul tasting poor show   the room was clean the bed comfortable and the internet service acceptable

The Gore exquisite outstanding This is a Five Star hotel of the utmost Quality It is a boutique hotel independent The standard of care and product is simply tremendous We stay in a variety of hotels at the moment for our work in music and we have seen it all At the time of writing this is the very best Special mention to the lovely reception team who offered a free upgrade most unexpected and Trevor the very kind Conci rge a true gentleman We had a classic superior double room with bath but even this simple double room was outstanding I have no doubt all the rooms are excellent Room Service Breakfast American Pancakes was superb I have nothing negative to relate P S unlike a recent stay at another London hotel the Gore released any pre authorised funds immediately to my card You notice the difference in customer care

From arriving at the hotel the staff were friendly and helpful The guy behind the bar Mickayl i think was brilliant and helped with best directions to where we wanted to go The breakfast was good and plentiful and the staff in here were pleasant The hotel itself was in a good location 5 mins from Earls Court Tube station which was great for getting around London We were on the 5th floor of the hotel and it was very quiet the bathroom could have done with some tlc on the paintwork but nothing major the bar prices were reasonable with a pint of lager around 4 50 so not bad for London There is a Sainsburys just down the road which was great when we were unpacking on the Friday to find we had no toothpaste with us

Most staff were wonderful and the hotel was fantastic overall However that one member of staff really did spur the taste of mecure   There were a couple of issues that were solved apologetically The lady who brought our breakfast was fantastically attentive However the reception lady when we checked out was not despite me passing on feedback that her colleague was so helpful her response was I m sorry about that Please listen to your customers when they are giving you feedback you arrogant woman

There were several rooms of disruptive guests school children on my floor Each day sometimes multiple times I had to call the front desk to report door slamming running back and forth in the hall loud noise this happened on Sat night until 11 45 pm and began Sunday morning at 7 30 am The hotel staff person on Sunday night said I have asked the children to be quiet but they haven t done it so there is nothing we can do I could not believe this Finally I asked her to meet me on my floor in the hallway and we were going to have a conversation with the children and their teacher adult accompanying them and get this resolved So we did just that The teacher was very attentive and he did stop the noise I just wonder why the hotel staff did not take such initiative on Friday Saturday and Sunday So frustrating and disruptive I mentioned that I was certain there was a hotel policy on unnecessary noise and disruption but she didn t respond only saying that they had asked them to be quiet and they hadn t done it so they could do nothing This Saturday night staff person said she would have the manager contact me I have received no word from the manager I also told this staff person that I would have to go on social media and state what happened as I wouldn t want this to happen to anyone else and she said go ahead I don t care I think this might be the bottom line SHE DIDN T CARE NOR DID THE HOTEL

This hotel punches well above its weight It s like they took my list of things that really annoy me about hotels and made sure none of them were present I was incredibly impressed with this hotel Firstly it s not silly money for the location That got me there in the first place and it exceeded my expectations in every respect Check in is excellent Lots of staff all of whom were friendly efficient This is repeated throughout the hotel It s properly crewed and service is excellent in every department Early check in was handled charmingly and luggage stored properly Unusual for many hotels Our room was superb Small but very high quality They managed to fit in a proper sized desk decent armchair Nespresso machine and all the usual gubbins decent sized bed with great pillows decent linen The aircon actually cooled the room too The hotel is in an ugly purpose built concrete block and the huge advantage of this was the rooms are very quiet Interior d cor is great all comfy and great ambience Bathroom was a decent size and most unusually for hotels these days had enough shelving for all your stuff and a mirror that was actually lit enough to use Breakfast is impressive Firstly you get your tea and coffee within seconds of sitting down It s always a pleasant shock when that happens It s well staffed the food is kept stocked and the quality is high They ve got all the usual stuff and the quality of the cooked breakfast was great This place was a treat Really enjoyed our stay and will definitely go back

First of all during my 4 days stay 3 times a keycard did not work and i needed to go down to reprogram it Also for me the pictures in the internet looked much nicer than actually the hotel was I have stayed in many of the 4 star hotels in London and that was so far the worst of it the room was so tight that i couldnt even lay all my bags on the floor nor move freely beside the bed and the wall This is not a 4 star but more 3 star hotel and i expected way more from well known hotel chain I also cant understand that if there is a tv in the room why it isnt on the wall so you can watch it straight from the bed but the tv is on a table what is on the left near the up side of bed so the only option to watch the tv is to stand aside the bed but you cant watch it while being in bed Also the wind blew in from window and i couldnt close it correctly and the air heater either gave too much heat or no heat at all i assume the tunable swich did not work correctly Also the bathroom was so tiny that you cant sit in a tolilet and use the facility correctly I had great expectations on that hotel an took a lot of time of booking but to be honest the room was rather disappointing  I liked the location and staff was friendly and professional

Our room was very small we had a standard double room which had about 30cm on one side of the double bed and only enough room at the foot of the bed to shuffle sideways around The small desk table against the wall had the tea tray and other items so not really any space to put anything down We considered asking about the possibility of an upgrade to a larger room but decided we could cope for the 4 nights we were there We didn t spend much time in the room as we had planned a busy trip and the one evening we returned early we enjoyed some time in the bar lounge area so the room was adequate for our needs Do check the hotel website if you are concerned about room size as there is very detailed information available before booking Our main concern was the size of the bed as we re used to a big bed Despite being smaller than we re used to the bed was very comfortable with the most comfortable pillows A real bonus in a small room was to have a bath shower in a very decent sized bathroom If the size of the room matters to you then certainly consider the information but please don t be put off as this is a great hotel   The Mercure Hotel is in a great location within easy reach of many attractions especially the Natural History Museum and the V A The hotel reception bar area are clean modern and inviting Nothing was any trouble for the really friendly helpful staff Take advantage of their offer to book a cab if you need one as the company they use is great Very pleasant breakfast room with a good selection of food options on offer A very comfortable bed with great pillows and the hotel even supplies ear plugs in case you aren t used to traffic noise although we were barely aware of the nearby road One of us doesn t like using the tube but we were able to get buses easily from Cromwell Road or Kensington High Street and really enjoyed seeing the sights on route to our destinations Very good value hotel and we would definitely stay again

around only a one side tabled bed and bare minimum lighting which I think is normal for London hotels however there were other things which made my life miserable Starting from TV and its remote which difficulty during communication Following which we found out that the remote was in operational and had to be changed It was the first time in my life that a hotel did not activate my wake up alarm despite calling them to inform about it in advance The wake up call came after 20 minutes of the set time Another first for me was that the room service personnel did not bother picking up used towels from my room after getting the room cleaned up Unfortunately the air conditioning stayed very low to keep me sweating at many times Upon checking the temperature it was set at 5 degree with maximum cooling but I was mostly uncomfortable throughout the night  I chose Mercure hotel as it was the first time I was travelling to London and I wanted to stay in a central location This would turn out to be helpful in my commute and enable me to get my work done easily as well Upon entering the hotel the reception made the check in process quite easy but after getting into the lift things were quite messy Some good things about the facility is that it has a good lobby where you can sit and spend some time The tables outside on the road also makes for a good view The hotel is located close by Gloucester road tube station with a 5 minute walk Also it is close to favorite tourist destinations like Natural History museum Victoria and Albert gallery Harrods and some other famous places

For the night I was there could not fault it as I had no issues arise in my stay there really Only minor point for anyone staying there is they do not have a main full restaurant there so would need to take a five to ten minute walk either way to find food establishments but does serve food like a selection of fresh made sandwiches during the day or a pizza which is their limit but what they did offer did taste nice and was quite fair for the money considering London prices can be   Hotel felt quite quaint and not overcrowded for a city hotel and staff were very friendly professional and efficient at their roles within the hotel made it a hassle free stay for the night They even looked after my bags on the second day when I checked out to go to a exhibition and picked them up on the way through at the end of the day Also as standard they have a mini bar fridge which comes with complimentary soft drinks two bottles of water but has no sensors so you can stock up on cold beverages of your own from round the corner at the local Sainsbury s without fear of being charged when you take a drink out was quite nice to have in a standard room It seemed nothing was to much of an effort for them no matter what you asked for or needed In short with my one nights stay I was impressed with the Mercure as the standard rooms are a normal size affair not to small but not yet massive with a nice bath shower set up and comfortable beds with nice pillows Could not fault the cleanliness through out my stay either
